name: Balfa with Dal
recipeIngredient: 225 g Mung beans, split, moong dal
recipeInstructions:
1. In a heavy-bottomed pot or pan, combine the mung beans and water. Bring to a boil.
2. Add half of the chopped onions, salt, sugar, green chilies, bay leaves, and cinnamon sticks to the boiling beans.
3. In a separate bowl, mix flour with salt and rub in ghee or oil until a smooth dough forms.
4. Divide the dough into 10 equal parts and shape them into round balls.
5. Drop the dough balls into the boiling bean mixture and reduce heat. Cook for 25 minutes, then remove the cooked dough balls one by one using tongs or a spatula.
6. Place the cooked dough balls in a greased baking dish. Bake in a preheated oven at 375°F (190°C) until the skin cracks and turns light brown.
7. Press each dough ball lightly in the center with your thumb, then break it gently. Add 1 or 2 teaspoons of ghee into each bafla. They can also be eaten without ghee.
8. In a separate pan, heat oil and fry the remaining chopped onions until lightly browned.
9. Pour the cooked dal over the fried onions and stir well. Let it boil for 2-3 minutes.
10. Serve the baflas with the hot dal and sprinkle with chopped coriander.
Note: Ensure the dal is poured over the baflas, breaking them completely. Additional vegetable dishes or chutney can also be served alongside.
